comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
STATUS (session pause, worktree .claude/worktrees/sail-shoe-smear, branch worktree-sail-shoe-smear, 5 commits ahead of origin/main c698cd243):

CLOSED (5/10 children):
- 3ee0 community health files — signed completion-verifier PASS (commit ed6dcfbb1)
- b593 pyproject metadata — FORCE-CLOSED, uncertified (commit 2c1b2f403). Completion-verifier could not RUN: rebar-internal plumbing bug (verify model returns top-level JSON array; validate_to 'got list'), reproduced 5/5. Tracked as bug dash-lure-slag (discovered_from this epic). NOTE: because this child is closed-but-uncertified, the epic's own completion gate will read certifiable=false — re-close b593 cleanly (rebar reopen b593 then transition closed) once dash-lure-slag is fixed, OR accept an unsigned epic close.
- e110 run_workflow durability docstrings — signed PASS (commit 3ed767d7c)
- fce6 MCP ToolAnnotations on all 45 tools + mcp floor >=1.9 — signed PASS (commit a38b61fae)
- b49a server.json env contract + MCP_ENV_VARS SoT + CI drift-guard — signed PASS (commit af2d2ee6a)

OPEN (5/10 remaining): 95dc supply-chain CI (plan-review PASS already earned), 3a10 typed models, 830d api-stability doc, d063 scale-envelope doc, 3bf6 README quickstart. All plan-reviews returned PASS with 0 blocking; advisories triaged inline per commit.

INFRA: tickets-branch auto-push has been failing (shared-remote merge conflicts) all session — local store is intact; run 'rebar fsck-recover' / reconcile before final landing.

LANDING: not started. Multi-story epic -> per CONTRIBUTING.md landing goes through Gerrit (feature branch or single squash change).

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: STATUS (final): ALL 10/10 children CLOSED. This session closed the remaining 5: 95dc (supply-chain CI: CodeQL + gating pip-audit — signed PASS), 3a10 (schema-derived TypedDicts + drift gate — signed PASS), 830d (api-stability doc — signed PASS), d063 (scale-envelope doc w/ measured numbers — signed PASS), 3bf6 (README Quickstart — signed PASS). Plan-review claim gate was intermittently rate-limited (429/INDETERMINATE); resolved by ~8-min spacing between attempts (no --force used; all 5 earned real PASS attestations). b593 remains closed-but-uncertified (bug dash-lure-slag, completion-verifier rejects a top-level JSON array); per decision we ACCEPT an UNSIGNED epic close and leave dash-lure-slag as tracked follow-up (discovered_from this epic). Landing: multi-story -> Gerrit feature branch per CONTRIBUTING §4.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: FORCE_CLOSE: close gate(s) bypassed by user approval — no completion/signature attestation was signed. Reason: "Accepted unsigned per owner decision: all 10 children implemented + closed (9 signed); only b593 is uncertified because bug dash-lure-slag (completion-verifier rejects a top-level JSON array) blocks its attestation. b593's pyproject implementation is complete and verified; dash-lure-slag remains open as tracked follow-up (discovered_from this epic). Fixing it is out-of-scope for this OSS-readiness epic.". Session: 25d6ed9e3.

compiled_state.description:
## Context

An external architectural review graded rebar's engineering core near
"high-assurance internal platform" quality (A-/B+ across architecture,
concurrency, CLI, MCP, CI) but its **public OSS/community packaging** a full tier
lower (C+ community readiness, B docs, B library-API clarity). Every factual
claim in that review was verified against the tree: no `SECURITY.md` /
`CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md` / issue+PR templates; `server.json` advertises only 3 env
vars and the *deprecated* `REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_RECONCILE_LIVE`, omitting
`REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_LLM` + `REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_JIRA_SYNC`; MCP tools carry no
behavior annotations; `pyproject` has no authors/keywords/classifiers/urls; no
SAST / dependency-vuln scanning; no published API-stability policy or scale
envelope; the `run_workflow` docstring oversells a `daemon`-thread as
"resumable on crash"; and the `rebar.*` facade returns bare dicts with no
documented typed contract.

This epic closes the **OSS-surface gap only** — the packaging, community,
manifest-accuracy, docs, and library-contract layer — WITHOUT touching the
functional/correctness roadmap already tracked in
`docs/oss-comparison-and-remediation.md` (HLC ordering, authenticated identity,
git-gc growth). Scope was locked interactively with the maintainer; three heavy
engineering items were deliberately excluded (see Scope · OUT).

## Success Criteria

- [ ] A newcomer landing on the GitHub mirror finds the standard community
  health files and a short golden-path onboarding, and understands that code
  lands via Gerrit (GitHub is a read-only mirror).
- [ ] The MCP `server.json` manifest matches the real config/env contract and is
  protected from silent re-drift by a CI gate.
- [ ] MCP clients can render safe UI from tool behavior hints
  (read-only / destructive / idempotent / open-world).
- [ ] `nava-rebar` presents complete, discoverable PyPI metadata (authors,
  keywords, classifiers, project URLs).
- [ ] Supply-chain posture adds SAST (CodeQL) + dependency-vulnerability scanning
  as CI checks and documents the PyPI attestations the release already emits —
  adapted to the read-only-mirror model (no dead-end auto-PRs, no misleading
  Scorecard badge).
- [ ] The public contract surface (CLI / JSON schemas / Python facade / MCP
  tools / event schema / config keys) has a published stability matrix.
- [ ] An honest scale envelope (counts, throughput, latency, git-growth,
  maintenance) is published with representative measured numbers.
- [ ] The `run_workflow` durability docstring states true crash semantics.
- [ ] The `rebar.*` facade has a documented typed return contract (schema-derived
  TypedDicts, drift-gated) and a documented stable exception surface.

## Scope

### IN (10 workstreams, decomposed into the child stories below)
1. Community health files (SECURITY, CODE_OF_CONDUCT, issue/PR templates, SUPPORT).
2. Fix `server.json` env/config contract + add a CI drift-guard.
3. Annotate MCP tools with behavior hints.
4. Enrich `pyproject` package metadata.
5. Supply-chain CI hardening (CodeQL + pip-audit + alerts + attestation docs).
6. API-stability matrix doc.
7. Scale-envelope doc.
8. External onboarding quickstart (single-sourced; defers to `rebar --help`).
9. `run_workflow` durability-docstring honesty fix.
10. Typed public library models (schema-derived TypedDicts, drift-gated).

### OUT (explicitly deferred; not part of this epic)
- **Durable workflow runner** (lease/reaper/resume worker) — separate effort;
  only the docstring-honesty fix (WS9) is in scope here.
- **Batched-commit bulk import** — touches the invariant-gated write path;
  belongs with the functional-remediation plan.
- **PR->Gerrit mirror bot** — ongoing service, contested vs. the Gerrit gate;
  the redirect PR template (WS1) delivers the cheap substitute.
- **OpenSSF Scorecard badge/workflow** — dropped: Code-Review/Branch-Protection
  sub-scores read low BY DESIGN on a Gerrit-gated mirror.
- **CI benchmark harness** and **SBOM** — deferred; docs-only scale envelope
  (WS7) and CI-check-only supply chain (WS5) instead.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All 10 in-scope workstreams are decomposed into child stories/tasks parented
  to this epic, each with its own `## Acceptance Criteria`.
- [ ] Each child ticket passes the plan-review gate (run in an LLM-enabled
  environment) and, on completion, the completion-verifier gate.
- [ ] No child ticket modifies the invariant-gated write path or introduces a
  new runtime dependency into the deliberately-tiny core
  (`pyyaml`/`jsonschema`/`referencing`); the typed-models work is static-only.
- [ ] The OUT items above are NOT implemented under this epic.
- [ ] `make lint` and the existing CI gates remain green after each child lands.
compiled_state.title: OSS-readiness surface: community files, MCP manifest, packaging, docs, API contract
